content: The orthographic variant "reflexion" (the less common spelling of the linguistic term "reflection") can act as a conceptual mirror that exposes gaps in current large language model (LLM) self-reflection mechanisms. Just as the spelling difference draws attention to how we conceptualize the act of reflection itself, LLMs can be prompted to identify "spelling-like inconsistencies" across their multiple reasoning attempts to improve self-criticism, building on the multi-perspective self-reflection framework of existing LLM research.
